pests.name: Gram Pod Borer
pests.damageSymptoms: Larva bores pods, head inside, eats developing seeds; #1 yield loss
pests.etl: 1-2 larvae/plant OR 5% pod damage OR 8 moths/trap/night

prevention: Intercrop with sorghum/maize (bird perch + parasitoid harbour)
monitoring: Helicoverpa pheromone traps 4-5/acre; economic threshold on catches
cultural: Shake plants to dislodge larvae onto ground

pests.name: Pod Fly
pests.iracRotationNote: Systemic + contact at podding; hard to reach inside pod
pests.damageSymptoms: Maggot feeds inside seed; no external hole till exit; shrivelled/partly-eaten seed

pests.name: Plume Moth
pests.damageSymptoms: Larva scrapes pod surface & bores, feeds on seeds; frass at feeding site

pests.name: Pod Sucking Bugs
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucks developing seeds -> shrivelled, discoloured, poor germination

monitoring: Count bugs on pods at dawn

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
diseases.symptoms: Progressive wilting, partial/full plant yellowing; internal black-brown vascular streak in split stem

monitoring: Scout wilting; split stem for vascular browning
cultural: Crop rotation with non-host; intercrop sorghum reduces wilt
biological: Trichoderma viride soil application with FYM @ 2 kg/acre

diseases.name: Sterility Mosaic Disease (SMD)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; target eriophyid mite vector
diseases.symptoms: Bushy pale mosaic foliage, no flowering/pods (sterility), stunting

monitoring: Scout bushy mosaic; check for eriophyid mites

diseases.name: Phytophthora Blight
diseases.symptoms: Dark stem lesions, girdling, collapse; worse in waterlogged patches

prevention: Well-drained fields; ridge planting; resistant lines
monitoring: Scout stem lesions after heavy rain
cultural: Improve drainage; avoid low spots; rotation

diseases.name: Dry Root Rot
diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting in dry spell, shredded bark with black microsclerotia

pests.name: Shoot Fly
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart in seedlings (central shoot dries, pulls out easily)

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart, stem tunnelling

pests.name: White Grub
pests.damageSymptoms: Grubs feed on roots -> sudden wilting/drying of plants in patches (sandy soils)

prevention: Deep summer ploughing to expose grubs
monitoring: Dig root zone in wilting patches; count grubs
Light traps at monsoon onset to gauge beetle emergence
cultural: Community light-trapping of adult beetles on first rains
Collect beetles from host trees (neem/ber) at dusk
biological: Metarhizium anisopliae soil application @ 2 kg/acre with FYM

pests.name: Grey Weevil / Blister Beetle
pests.damageSymptoms: Weevil notches leaf margins; blister beetle feeds on flowers/pollen at earhead

cultural: Hand-collect blister beetles (wear gloves; they blister skin)

diseases.name: Downy Mildew / Green Ear
diseases.fracRotationNote: Seed treatment Metalaxyl is primary; foliar Metalaxyl+Mancozeb
diseases.symptoms: Chlorosis + white downy underside; ear head converts to leafy green structures (green ear); stunting

monitoring: Scout for downy patches & green-ear at booting

chemical.note: Early foliar; once green ear forms, no cure

diseases.name: Ergot / Sugary Disease
diseases.symptoms: Pink sticky honeydew oozing from florets -> hard black sclerotia (toxic to livestock/humans)

prevention: Clean seed free of sclerotia (float in 10% salt solution to remove)
monitoring: Watch honeydew on ears at flowering

diseases.name: Smut
diseases.symptoms: Individual grains replaced by larger green-black sori (smut balls)

cultural: Rogue & destroy smutted ears (bag before removal)

diseases.name: Rust
diseases.symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaves, premature drying

pests.name: Shoot & Fruit Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 6 -> 28 -> 28(diff); okra picked every 2-3 days so prefer low-PHI molecules
pests.damageSymptoms: Drooping bored shoots (early); bore holes in fruit with frass -> unmarketable

monitoring: Earias pheromone traps 8/acre; weekly damage grading
cultural: Clip & destroy bored shoots + fruits at each picking
biological: Trichogramma chilonis @ 20,000/acre; Bt @ 400 g/acre; conserve Bracon

pests.name: Whitefly (Yellow Vein Mosaic vector)
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucking + transmits Yellow Vein Mosaic (yellow vein network, pale deformed fruit) - NO cure

pests.name: Jassids / Leafhopper
pests.damageSymptoms: Leaf-edge yellowing, cupping, hopper burn, stunting

prevention: Seed treatment Imidacloprid 600 FS @ 8-10 ml/kg (protects 30-40 days)
monitoring: Nymph counts on lower leaf surface; yellow sticky traps

pests.name: Red Spider Mite
pests.damageSymptoms: Stippling, bronzing, webbing underside in hot-dry spells

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damageSymptoms: Colonies on tender shoots, honeydew, sooty mould

diseases.name: Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus (YVMV)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; target whitefly vector + resistant variety
diseases.symptoms: Network of yellow veins -> whole leaf yellows; pale, deformed, unmarketable fruit

cultural: Remove weed hosts (Abutilon, Croton); reflective mulch

diseases.name: Enation Leaf Curl Virus (OELCV)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; whitefly vector management (as YVMV)
diseases.symptoms: Leaf curling, thickened veins, enations (warty outgrowths) on underside, stunting

prevention: Tolerant varieties; rogue infected plants early
cultural: Remove weed hosts; reflective mulch; border crop

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves (late crop), yellowing, defoliation
diseases.favourableConditions: Warm dry days, cool nights, late-season

diseases.name: Cercospora / Alternaria Leaf Spot
diseases.symptoms: Angular/irregular brown leaf spots, sooty growth, defoliation

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
diseases.symptoms: Yellowing, wilting, vascular browning, stunting

prevention: Seed treatment Trichoderma @ 10 g/kg OR Carbendazim @ 2 g/kg
monitoring: Split stem for vascular browning in wilting plants
cultural: Crop rotation; organic matter; well-drained soil
biological: Trichoderma viride soil application with FYM

pests.name: Shoot & Fruit Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 6 -> 28 -> 28(diff); avoid OPs that flare mites; respect PHI (continuous picking)
pests.damageSymptoms: Wilting drooping shoots (early); bore holes in fruit plugged with frass -> unmarketable

monitoring: Leucinodes pheromone traps 8-10/acre; weekly damage grading
cultural: Clip & destroy bored shoots + damaged fruits weekly (single most effective step)

pests.name: Jassids / Leafhopper
pests.damageSymptoms: Leaf-margin yellowing, cupping, hopper burn; sap sucking

monitoring: Yellow sticky traps; nymph counts on lower leaf surface

pests.name: Whitefly (Little Leaf phytoplasma spread)
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucking + honeydew; associated with little-leaf/leaf-disorders spread

pests.name: Epilachna Beetle (Hadda)
pests.damageSymptoms: Adults & grubs scrape leaf -> skeletonised, lace-like leaves

pests.name: Red Spider Mite
pests.damageSymptoms: Stippling, bronzing, webbing on leaf underside; hot-dry weather

prevention: Avoid dusty field edges & moisture stress
monitoring: Hand-lens check of leaf underside for mites/webbing

diseases.name: Phomopsis Blight & Fruit Rot
diseases.symptoms: Grey leaf spots with dark margin; dieback; soft sunken circular fruit rot with pycnidia

cultural: Remove infected fruit/debris; rotation; staking to keep fruit off soil

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO chemical cure; resistant rootstock + soil management
diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting without yellowing; vascular browning; milky ooze from cut stem in water
diseases.favourableConditions: Warm wet soils, waterlogging, root injury

cultural: Soil solarization; bleaching powder @ 12 kg/acre; rogue plants

chemical.note: Limits spread only; not a cure

diseases.name: Little Leaf
diseases.symptoms: Tiny bunched pale leaves, shortened internodes, sterile flowers, no fruit

prevention: Net-raised seedlings; rogue infected plants early
cultural: Remove weed hosts; destroy infected plants

diseases.name: Damping-off (nursery)
diseases.symptoms: Pre/post-emergence seedling collapse; water-soaked collar rot in nursery

prevention: Raised nursery beds; Trichoderma seed treatment @ 10 g/kg
cultural: Soil solarization of beds; thin sowing

pests.name: Diamondback Moth (DBM)
pests.iracRotationNote: #1 cabbage pest; Bt/Spinosad first, rotate 5 -> 28 -> 6; sticker mandatory
pests.damageSymptoms: Windowpane scraping on leaves; bores into head

prevention: Mustard trap-crop border (proven for cabbage DBM)
biological: Bt kurstaki @ 400 g/acre; Cotesia plutellae; Trichogramma

pests.name: Cabbage Head Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Spray BEFORE head formation (unreachable once inside); rotate 28 / 6
pests.damageSymptoms: Bores growing point & head, webs with frass; deforms head

pests.name: Cabbage Butterfly
pests.damageSymptoms: Large green larvae defoliate, leave frass; skeletonise outer leaves

monitoring: Scout undersides for yellow egg clusters & larvae

pests.name: Aphids & Painted Bug
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 4A -> 29; painted bug worst at seedling
pests.damageSymptoms: Aphids: colonies + sooty mould; painted bug: sap sucking, seedling wilting

diseases.name: Black Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Hot-water seed treatment + Copper+Streptocycline; #1 cabbage disease
diseases.symptoms: V-shaped marginal yellowing, blackened veins, head rot

prevention: Hot-water seed treatment (50C/30 min); clean seed; tolerant varieties
cultural: Rotation 2-3 yr; drip; avoid working wet plants; rogue plants

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.symptoms: Yellow patches upper, downy underside; on outer wrapper leaves & head

monitoring: Scout underside in cool humid spells

diseases.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
diseases.symptoms: Target-board dark concentric spots on leaves & head

diseases.name: Sclerotinia / Bottom Rot
diseases.symptoms: Watery soft rot of lower leaves/head base; white cottony mycelium + black sclerotia (Sclerotinia); brown bottom rot (Rhizoctonia)

cultural: Deep ploughing to bury sclerotia; rotation; avoid waterlogging

pests.name: Thrips
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 5 -> 28 -> 2B; in polyhouse pair with predatory mites
pests.damageSymptoms: Scarring, silvering, leaf curl, flower/fruit scarring; T. parvispinus causes severe tip die-back

monitoring: Blue sticky traps; flower shake test
cultural: Remove weed hosts; rogue heavily curled plants
biological: Amblyseius swirskii predatory mite (excellent in protected culture)

pests.name: Mites (broad + two-spotted)
pests.damageSymptoms: Broad mite: leaf curl/bronzing; two-spotted: stippling & webbing

pests.name: Aphids & Whitefly (virus vectors)
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucking, honeydew, sooty mould; transmit mosaic & leaf-curl viruses

cultural: Remove weed hosts; rogue virus plants

pests.name: Fruit Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 28 -> 6; respect PHI in protected culture
pests.damageSymptoms: Bores fruit, feeds inside, entry rot

diseases.name: Anthracnose / Fruit Rot
diseases.symptoms: Sunken circular lesions on fruit with concentric rings; die-back

prevention: Seed treatment Carbendazim+Thiram @ 3 g/kg; clean seed
cultural: Drip not overhead irrigation; remove infected fruit; rotation

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery growth underside, yellow patches upper, heavy leaf drop
diseases.favourableConditions: Protected-culture humidity swings, warm dry air

monitoring: Scout underside; act on first patches (spreads fast in polyhouse)

diseases.name: Damping-off & Root Rot
diseases.symptoms: Seedling collapse; wilting with root/collar rot; Phytophthora crown rot in wet media

prevention: Well-drained media; Trichoderma seed & media treatment

diseases.name: Bacterial Spot
diseases.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots -> dark scabby lesions on leaves & fruit

cultural: Drip irrigation; avoid working wet plants; rotation

diseases.name: Mosaic / Leaf Curl Viruses
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; net exclusion + vector control (big polyhouse advantage)
diseases.symptoms: Mosaic mottling, leaf curl/distortion, stunting, fruit deformation

prevention: Insect-proof 40-50 mesh net; tolerant hybrids; rogue infected plants
cultural: Remove weed hosts; sanitation between crops

pests.name: Diamondback Moth (DBM)
pests.iracRotationNote: MOST resistance-prone pest; start Bt/Spinosad, rotate 5 -> 28 -> 6 strictly; always add sticker
pests.damageSymptoms: Larvae scrape leaf underside -> windowpane patches; bore into curd/heart

prevention: Mustard trap-crop border (20:2 rows) - highly effective for DBM
monitoring: DBM pheromone traps 10/acre; weekly larval counts
cultural: Overhead sprinkler irrigation dislodges & kills larvae

pests.name: Tobacco Caterpillar / Cabbage Butterfly
pests.damageSymptoms: Gregarious larvae skeletonise leaves; grown larvae defoliate; Pieris big green larvae

monitoring: Spodoptera pheromone traps; scout egg masses
biological: SlNPV @ 100 LE/acre; Bt @ 400 g/acre; Trichogramma + Bracon

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damageSymptoms: Dense colonies on leaves/curd, honeydew, sooty mould, distortion

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.symptoms: Yellow patches upper, white-grey downy growth underside; black speckling on curd
diseases.favourableConditions: Cool humid weather, dew, dense crop

prevention: Tolerant varieties; wider spacing; clean seed
monitoring: Scout underside in cool humid spells
cultural: Airflow; drip not overhead; residue destruction

diseases.name: Black Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Hot-water seed treatment + Copper+Streptocycline; #1 bacterial disease
diseases.symptoms: V-shaped yellow lesions from leaf margin inward; blackened veins; systemic rot
diseases.favourableConditions: Warm wet weather, overhead irrigation, injury

monitoring: Scout V-lesions from margins after rain
cultural: Crop rotation (2-3 yr non-crucifer); drip; avoid working wet plants; rogue plants

chemical.note: At first symptoms; no true cure

diseases.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
diseases.symptoms: Concentric-ring (target-board) dark spots on leaves & curd; seed-borne

prevention: Seed treatment Iprodione/Mancozeb @ 2-3 g/kg; clean seed

diseases.name: Damping-off & Sclerotinia Stalk Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Trichoderma nursery; Carbendazim drench for Sclerotinia
diseases.symptoms: Nursery seedling collapse; field: watery soft stalk rot with white cottony mycelium + black sclerotia

prevention: Raised beds; Trichoderma seed treatment; avoid over-watering
cultural: Wider spacing; drainage; deep ploughing to bury sclerotia; rotation

pests.name: Thrips (drives leaf curl / Murda)
pests.damageSymptoms: Upward leaf curl, puckering, silvery scarring; part of leaf-curl complex; scarred fruit

monitoring: Blue sticky traps 10/acre; tap young leaves over sheet
cultural: Remove weed hosts & early curled plants

pests.name: Yellow / Broad Mite
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 23 -> 6 -> sulphur; part of leaf-curl complex
pests.damageSymptoms: Downward curling & cupping of leaves, shiny bronze underside, elongated leaves, inverted-boat look

monitoring: Hand-lens underside inspection of young leaves

pests.name: Aphids (virus vector)
pests.damageSymptoms: Sap sucking, honeydew, sooty mould; transmits mosaic viruses

pests.name: Fruit Borer
pests.damageSymptoms: Bores into fruit, feeds on seeds; frass; fruit rot entry

cultural: Hand-collect larvae; remove bored fruits; bird perches

diseases.name: Anthracnose / Die-back & Fruit Rot
diseases.symptoms: Twig die-back from tip; sunken circular fruit lesions with concentric acervuli; ripe-fruit rot
diseases.favourableConditions: Warm humid weather, rain at fruiting

cultural: Prune dead twigs; remove infected fruit; rotation; avoid overhead irrigation
biological: Trichoderma seed treatment; Pseudomonas fluorescens spray

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Complex (Murda)
diseases.pathogen: Chilli Leaf Curl Virus (thrips/whitefly + mite driven)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; manage thrips+mite+whitefly vector complex
diseases.symptoms: Upward/downward leaf curl, puckering, bushy stunted plant, no fruit set
diseases.favourableConditions: High thrips/mite/whitefly activity, hot dry spells

monitoring: Blue+yellow sticky traps; scout curling early
cultural: Rogue infected plants; remove weed hosts

diseases.name: Damping-off & Wilt
diseases.symptoms: Seedling collapse in nursery; field wilting with root/collar rot

prevention: Raised beds; Trichoderma seed & nursery treatment

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery patches on leaf underside, yellow blotches upper, defoliation

pests.name: Fruit Fly
pests.iracRotationNote: Traps + bait are PRIMARY; cover-spray alone fails
pests.damageSymptoms: Female punctures young fruit to lay eggs; maggots rot fruit; oozing, deformed, dropping fruit
pests.etl: Trap-catch based; act on first catches

cultural: Collect & destroy fallen infested fruits DAILY (breaks cycle)
biological: Conserve Opius parasitoid; poison-bait relies on kill not bio

chemical.technical: Poison bait: jaggery/molasses 100 g/L + Malathion 50% EC 10 ml/L

pests.name: Red Pumpkin Beetle
pests.damageSymptoms: Orange adults eat cotyledons/young leaves (can wipe seedlings); grubs damage roots & ground-touching fruit

prevention: Early sowing; raise seedlings under net then transplant
cultural: Dust wood-ash on seedlings early morning
Handpick beetles; soil sanitation for grubs

pests.name: Aphids / Whitefly / Thrips (virus vectors)
pests.scientificName: Aphis gossypii / Bemisia tabaci / Thrips palmi
pests.damageSymptoms: Sap sucking, honeydew, sooty mould; transmit cucumber/yellow mosaic (no cure)

cultural: Remove weed hosts; rogue virus plants

pests.name: Red Spider Mite
pests.damageSymptoms: Stippling, bronzing, webbing on underside; hot-dry weather

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.symptoms: Angular yellow patches bounded by veins (upper), purplish downy growth underside
diseases.favourableConditions: High humidity, leaf wetness, cool nights

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery patches on both leaf surfaces & stem; premature drying
diseases.favourableConditions: Warm dry days, moderate humidity, poor airflow

monitoring: Scout upper leaves; act at first patches

chemical.note: Avoid on hot days / sensitive melons

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.symptoms: Water-soaked spots -> sunken lesions on leaves, stems, fruit; fruit rot

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt & Damping-off
diseases.symptoms: Seedling collapse (damping-off); field: yellowing, one-sided wilt, vascular browning

prevention: Seed treatment Trichoderma @ 10 g/kg; raised beds; grafting on resistant rootstock

diseases.name: Cucumber / Yellow Mosaic Virus
diseases.symptoms: Mosaic mottling, leaf distortion, stunting, warty deformed fruit

prevention: Tolerant hybrids; reflective/silver mulch; rogue infected plants
cultural: Remove weed hosts; border crop maize

pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.damageSymptoms: Larvae mine then web leaflets; brown blotches; burnt look in severe attack

cultural: Intercrop with pearl millet/cowpea to reduce buildup

pests.name: Thrips (also bud necrosis virus vector)
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 5 / 4A / fipronil; manage early for PBNV
pests.damageSymptoms: Silvering/curling of young leaves; transmits Bud Necrosis (ring spots, terminal necrosis) - no cure

monitoring: Blue sticky traps; tap young leaves over white sheet

pests.name: Tobacco Caterpillar / Red Hairy Caterpillar
pests.damageSymptoms: Gregarious young larvae skeletonise; grown larvae defoliate

monitoring: Spodoptera pheromone traps 4/acre; Amsacta light traps

pests.name: White Grub
pests.damageSymptoms: Grubs feed on roots/pods -> wilting patches, easy plant pull-out

prevention: Seed treatment Chlorpyriphos 20% EC @ 12 ml/kg OR Imidacloprid 600 FS @ 6 ml/kg
monitoring: Dig root zone; light traps at first rains for beetle emergence
cultural: Community light/bonfire trapping of adult beetles on host trees (neem, ber) after first monsoon showers

diseases.name: Early & Late Leaf Spot (Tikka)
diseases.symptoms: Early: brown spots with yellow halo (upper); Late: darker, no halo, rough underside; heavy defoliation

prevention: Tolerant varieties; crop rotation; timely sowing

diseases.name: Rust
diseases.fracRotationNote: Same advanced combos as tikka (tank-mix compatible)
diseases.symptoms: Orange-brown pustules on leaf underside; often with late leaf spot

monitoring: Scout underside pustules with leaf spot

diseases.name: Stem Rot / Collar Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Seed treatment (chemical or Trichoderma) is primary
diseases.symptoms: White mycelium + mustard-seed sclerotia at collar/stem base; sudden wilting; pod rot
diseases.favourableConditions: High soil moisture + temp, dense residue

prevention: Seed treatment Tebuconazole/Carbendazim @ 2-3 g/kg OR Trichoderma @ 4-10 g/kg
monitoring: Scout collar for white mycelium in wet patches
cultural: Deep ploughing; avoid dense residue; gypsum improves collar zone
biological: Trichoderma viride enriched FYM soil application

diseases.name: Bud Necrosis (PBND)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; target thrips vector early
diseases.symptoms: Chlorotic/necrotic ring spots, terminal bud necrosis, stunting, reduced pods

monitoring: Blue sticky traps; scout ring spots
cultural: Rogue infected plants; remove weed hosts

pests.name: Fall Armyworm (FAW)
pests.iracRotationNote: Cycle IRAC 5 -> 6 -> 28 across sprays; never repeat a group
pests.damageSymptoms: Ragged windowpane feeding + moist sawdust-like frass in whorl; inverted-Y on head + 4 dots on last segment

prevention: Seed treatment Cyantraniliprole 19.8% FS @ 6 ml/kg for early protection
monitoring: Scout whorls twice a week; grade damage
cultural: Sand+lime (9:1) or dry soil placed in whorl kills larvae
Deep summer ploughing to expose pupae
biological: Trichogramma pretiosum + Telenomus remus egg parasitoids
Spray Metarhizium rileyi / Beauveria bassiana 1x10^8 @ 400 g/acre
Bt kurstaki @ 400 g/acre for small larvae

chemical.technical: Poison bait (rice bran 10 kg + jaggery 2 kg + Thiodicarb 100 g)

pests.name: Stem Borer (Maize)
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart in young plants, shot-hole leaf rows, stem tunnelling

monitoring: Scout for shot-holes & dead hearts at 2-4 weeks

pests.name: Pink Stem Borer / Shoot Fly
pests.iracRotationNote: Seed treatment first line; avoid repeat neonics
pests.damageSymptoms: Shoot fly: dead heart in seedlings; Pink borer: stem tunnelling in Rabi/Spring maize

monitoring: Seedling dead-heart counts in first 21 days

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damageSymptoms: Colonies on whorl/tassel, honeydew + sooty mould, virus vector

diseases.name: Turcicum Leaf Blight
diseases.symptoms: Long cigar/boat-shaped grey-green necrotic lesions on leaves; heavy blighting reduces grain fill

monitoring: Scout lower leaves from knee-high stage
cultural: Destroy infected residue; avoid dense stand

diseases.name: Maydis / Southern Leaf Blight
diseases.fracRotationNote: Same chemistry as Turcicum; rotate M3 with 11+3
diseases.symptoms: Small tan diamond/rectangular lesions between veins, coalescing

monitoring: Scout leaves in warm humid spells

diseases.name: Banded Leaf & Sheath Blight
diseases.fracRotationNote: Validamycin / Hexaconazole directed to lower sheaths
diseases.symptoms: Concentric banded lesions on sheath & leaf, climbing; ear rot in severe cases
diseases.favourableConditions: Dense canopy, high humidity, warm temp

monitoring: Inspect lower sheaths in dense crop

diseases.name: Downy Mildew (incl. crazy top)
diseases.symptoms: Chlorotic streaks, white downy growth underside, stunting, malformed tassel (crazy top)
diseases.favourableConditions: High humidity, cool nights, waterlogging early

monitoring: Scout young plants for chlorotic streaks

diseases.name: Common Rust
diseases.symptoms: Cinnamon-brown powdery pustules on both leaf surfaces

pests.name: Whitefly (Yellow Mosaic Virus vector)
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucking + YMV (yellow mosaic, pod distortion) - viral, no cure

cultural: Remove weed hosts; border crop cereal

pests.name: Thrips (on flowers)
pests.damageSymptoms: Flower drop, malformed flowers, poor pod set

monitoring: Blue sticky traps; shake flowers over sheet

pests.name: Pod Borer complex
pests.damageSymptoms: Helicoverpa bores pods; Maruca webs flowers+pods and feeds inside

monitoring: Helicoverpa pheromone traps 4/acre; scout webbed flowers for Maruca

diseases.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus (YMV)
diseases.symptoms: Yellow-green mosaic, pod distortion, stunting, yield loss

cultural: Remove weed hosts; border crop cereal

diseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
diseases.symptoms: Circular spots with grey centre + reddish-brown margin, defoliation

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves/pods, premature drying
diseases.favourableConditions: Dry days + cool nights, late crop

diseases.name: Dry Root Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Seed treatment + cultural; avoid moisture stress
diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting, shredded bark at base with black microsclerotia

prevention: Seed treatment Trichoderma @ 10 g/kg OR Carbendazim @ 2 g/kg
monitoring: Split base of wilting plants for sclerotia

pests.name: Yellow Stem Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate diamides (IRAC 28) with Cartap (IRAC 14); never repeat a group back-to-back
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart in vegetative stage; white ear head (empty panicle) in reproductive stage
pests.etl: 5% dead hearts OR 1 egg mass/m2 OR 1 moth/m2

prevention: Synchronised community planting to break the cycle
monitoring: Pheromone traps (Scirpophaga lure) 5/acre for moth flush
Weekly scouting for dead hearts + egg masses on leaf tips
cultural: Clip seedling leaf tips at transplant to remove egg masses
biological: Release Trichogramma japonicum @ 20,000/acre (1 lakh/ha) weekly from 30 DAT, 4-6 releases

chemical.dose: 8 kg/acre broadcast in 2-5 cm standing water
chemical.note: Granular at 30-35 DAT on dead-heart appearance
Long residual, safe to natural enemies
Spray at ETL / white-ear risk at booting
Alternate with Cartap to manage resistance

pests.name: Brown Plant Hopper (BPH)
pests.iracRotationNote: NEVER use synthetic pyrethroids or excess N (causes resurgence); rotate 9B / 4A / 29
pests.damageSymptoms: Hopper burn (circular patches of dried plants), sooty mould, insects clustered at plant base

prevention: Maintain 30 cm alleys every 2-3 m for aeration & spray access
monitoring: Tap base of hills over water, count dislodged hoppers weekly
cultural: Drain field for 3-4 days at early infestation
biological: Conserve mirid bug Cyrtorhinus lividipennis (key predator), spiders, Lycosa

chemical.note: IGR, hits nymphs; direct spray to base

pests.name: Leaf Folder
pests.damageSymptoms: Larvae fold leaf longitudinally, scrape green tissue leaving white papery streaks
pests.etl: 1-2 freshly damaged leaves/hill OR 10% damaged leaves

monitoring: Count folded/scraped leaves weekly; check flag leaf near heading

pests.name: Gundhi Bug / Rice Earhead Bug
pests.iracRotationNote: Short-residual OPs/pyrethroids only at heading; single well-timed spray
pests.damageSymptoms: Sucks milky grains -> chaffy/discoloured grain with pin-point spots; foul smell in field
pests.etl: 1 bug/hill OR 5 bugs/m2 at milk stage

prevention: Synchronised planting so heading is uniform (avoids continuous food)
biological: Conserve spiders and reduced-viid predators; limited bio options at grain stage

chemical.note: Use only at heading; avoid earlier (BPH risk)

diseases.name: Blast (leaf + neck)
diseases.fracRotationNote: Don't repeat Tricyclazole (FRAC 16.1) all season; rotate with strobilurin+triazole (11+3)
diseases.symptoms: Spindle/eye-shaped spots, grey centre + brown margin on leaves; blackening & breaking at neck/node (neck blast = yield killer)
diseases.favourableConditions: 20-28C, cloudy, high humidity, dew, excess/late nitrogen

prevention: Avoid excess & late N; split N doses
monitoring: Scout leaf spots from tillering; watch weather (dew, cloud)
cultural: Avoid dense planting & standing-water N flushes

diseases.name: Sheath Blight
diseases.symptoms: Greenish-grey water-soaked lesions on sheath near waterline -> snake-skin banded pattern climbing up
diseases.favourableConditions: Dense canopy, high N, continuous flooding, high humidity

monitoring: Inspect sheath at waterline from max-tillering
biological: Trichoderma harzianum @ 400 g/acre; Pseudomonas fluorescens spray

diseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight (BLB)
diseases.fracRotationNote: No fungicide cure; copper + antibiotic to limit spread
diseases.symptoms: Yellow wavy margins drying from leaf tip downward; kresek (seedling wilt)
diseases.favourableConditions: Rain, wind, flooding, high N, wounded leaves

cultural: Avoid flow of water from infected field

diseases.name: Brown Spot
diseases.symptoms: Oval brown sesame-seed spots on leaves; grain discoloration; a stress/nutrient (K, Si) disease
diseases.favourableConditions: Nutrient-poor soils, drought stress, K deficiency

diseases.name: False Smut
diseases.fracRotationNote: Only the booting-stage preventive window works
diseases.symptoms: Velvety greenish-yellow spore balls replacing grains; turn olive-black
diseases.favourableConditions: High humidity/rain at flowering, high N

cultural: Destroy smut balls; avoid dense crop

chemical.note: Spray at boot leaf stage (once balls appear, no cure)

pests.name: Girdle Beetle
pests.damageSymptoms: Two ring-girdles on stem/petiole; portion above dries; larva tunnels stem

monitoring: Weekly scout for the twin girdle rings & drooping tops
cultural: Clip & destroy girdled shoots below lower ring (removes larva)

chemical.note: Combo for girdle beetle + defoliators together

pests.name: Defoliators (Tobacco caterpillar, Semilooper, Bihar hairy)
pests.scientificName: Spodoptera litura / Chrysodeixis acuta / Spilosoma obliqua
pests.damageSymptoms: Skeletonised/defoliated leaves; Spodoptera scrapes then defoliates; heavy podding-stage loss
pests.etl: 3 larvae/m row OR 25% defoliation pre-flowering / 15% at podding

prevention: Deep summer ploughing to kill Spodoptera pupae
monitoring: Scout undersides for egg masses & early larvae

pests.name: Whitefly (Yellow Mosaic Virus vector)
pests.iracRotationNote: Rotate 4A -> 12A -> 7C; manage for virus not just numbers
pests.damageSymptoms: Sap sucking + honeydew; transmits Yellow Mosaic (bright yellow mottling, stunting) which has NO cure
pests.etl: Manage early to prevent virus; do not wait for high counts

prevention: Rogue YMV plants at first sight

pests.name: Stem Fly
pests.damageSymptoms: Maggot tunnels petiole/stem; reddish tunnels; seedling mortality/stunting

monitoring: Split seedling stems to check tunnelling

diseases.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus (YMV)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO chemical cure; chemicals target whitefly vector
diseases.symptoms: Bright yellow mosaic/mottling, stunting, poor pod fill
diseases.favourableConditions: High whitefly activity, warm weather, nearby infected pulses

prevention: Rogue infected plants at first sight
monitoring: Yellow sticky traps 10/acre; scout mosaic weekly
cultural: Remove weed hosts; border crop maize/bajra
biological: Conserve Encarsia; Verticillium lecanii on whitefly

diseases.name: Rust
diseases.symptoms: Tan/reddish-brown pustules on leaf underside, rapid defoliation
diseases.favourableConditions: Cool humid weather, prolonged leaf wetness

diseases.name: Anthracnose / Pod Blight
diseases.symptoms: Dark sunken lesions on stem/pods/petioles; seed-borne; pod blight

prevention: Seed treatment Carbendazim+Thiram (1:1) @ 3 g/kg OR Trichoderma @ 5-10 g/kg

diseases.name: Charcoal Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: Mainly cultural + biological; avoid moisture stress
diseases.symptoms: Root/lower-stem grey with tiny black microsclerotia (charcoal dusting); wilting in dry spell

prevention: Avoid moisture stress; irrigate at flowering-podding
monitoring: Split lower stem for microsclerotia in wilting patches
cultural: Deep summer ploughing; crop rotation; organic matter
biological: Trichoderma viride soil application with FYM

pests.name: Early Shoot Borer
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart in young crop (up to 4 months); shoot pulls out with rotten smell

prevention: Sett treatment; avoid moisture stress early

chemical.note: Soil/base drench + light irrigation up to 90 days

pests.name: Top Borer
pests.iracRotationNote: Diamide GR + trichogramma; hard to reach in whorl
pests.damageSymptoms: Dead heart at top, bunchy top, shot holes in top leaves, red tunnels in top internodes
pests.etl: Broods appear Apr-Sep; act at first brood

monitoring: Scout for bunchy top & shot holes; pheromone traps
cultural: Remove egg masses on leaf midrib

pests.name: Pyrilla (Leafhopper)
pests.iracRotationNote: Conserve biocontrol FIRST; spray only in outbreak
pests.damageSymptoms: Nymphs+adults suck leaf sap; copious honeydew -> sooty mould blackens leaves; reduces sugar

monitoring: Leaf-underside counts; look for white egg masses & ectoparasite cocoons
cultural: Detrashing & removal of egg-laden lower leaves
biological: Conserve/augment Epiricania melanoleuca (ectoparasite) - highly effective; collect & release cocoons/egg masses from infested to new fields

chemical.note: Only in outbreak; spares if biocontrol active

pests.name: White Grub
pests.damageSymptoms: Grubs feed on roots -> yellowing, lodging, drying clumps in patches

monitoring: Dig root zone; light traps at monsoon onset
cultural: Community light-trapping of beetles on host trees at first rains

pests.name: Scale Insect / Mealybug
pests.iracRotationNote: Sett treatment + sanitation; systemic if severe
pests.damageSymptoms: Encrust nodes under leaf sheath, suck sap -> thin canes, low sugar; worse in ratoon & waterlogging

monitoring: Peel leaf sheath at nodes to inspect

diseases.name: Red Rot
diseases.fracRotationNote: No effective foliar cure; resistant variety + sett treatment is everything
diseases.symptoms: Drying of top leaves, reddening of internal pith with white crosswise patches, alcoholic/sour smell on splitting cane
diseases.favourableConditions: Waterlogging, monsoon, ratooning infected fields, susceptible varieties (Co-0238)

prevention: Resistant varieties (shift away from Co-0238 in affected belts -> Co-15023, Co-0118, Co-Lk-14201)
Disease-free seed cane from healthy nursery
monitoring: Split suspect canes for pith reddening + white patches; smell test
cultural: Improve drainage; crop rotation with rice/green manure
biological: Trichoderma harzianum sett dip + soil application

chemical.note: Seed cane treatment; moist hot-air therapy for nursery

diseases.name: Smut
diseases.symptoms: Black whip-like structure emerging from growing point; thin grassy tillers

monitoring: Scout for black whips; count affected clumps
cultural: Rogue whips (bag before removal to avoid spore spread)

diseases.name: Wilt
diseases.fracRotationNote: Resistant variety + healthy seed; chemical limited
diseases.symptoms: Gradual yellowing/withering, hollow light cane, internal purple-brown discoloration, boat-shaped pith
diseases.favourableConditions: Waterlogging alternating with drought, injured roots, red rot association

prevention: Resistant varieties; disease-free seed; avoid injury
monitoring: Split wilting canes for internal discoloration
cultural: Improve drainage; avoid moisture stress; rotation; rogue clumps

diseases.name: Grassy Shoot Disease (GSD)
diseases.fracRotationNote: NO cure; healthy seed + MHAT + rogue
diseases.symptoms: Profuse thin grassy tillers, chlorotic/white narrow leaves, no millable cane
diseases.favourableConditions: Infected seed cane, ratooning, leafhopper vectors

prevention: Moist Hot Air Treatment (MHAT 54C/4h) of seed cane
cultural: Rogue infected clumps; avoid ratooning infected; control leafhopper
